some.en: an amount or number of something that is not stated exactly

ex.en: I need some water.

some.en: used before a number to mean approximately

ex.en: Some 200 students attended the lecture.

southeast.en: the direction or region halfway between south and east

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the southeast.

southeast.en: situated in or facing the southeast

ex.en: Our house has a southeast-facing balcony.
The plane flew southeast toward the coast.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 해안을 향해 남동쪽으로 날아갔다.

space.cue: 물건을 둘 수 있는 빈 곳
space.en: an empty area that can be used for something

ex.en: There isn't enough space for another chair.
ex.ko: 의자를 하나 더 놓을 공간이 충분하지 않다.

space.en: the area outside the Earth's atmosphere where the stars and planets are

ex.en: Astronauts travel into space.

space.en: arrange things with gaps or intervals between them

ex.en: Space the seedlings about 30 centimeters apart.

special.en: different from what is usual, and often better

ex.en: This is a special day for us.

special.en: a specific dish or item offered temporarily, often at a lower price

ex.en: The soup of the day is today's special.

spiral.en: having a coiled, winding shape that spirals around a center

ex.en: They climbed the spiral staircase to the tower.
ex.ko: 그들은 탑으로 이어지는 나선형 계단을 올라갔다.

spiral.en: increase or decrease rapidly and uncontrollably

ex.en: The country's debt continued to spiral.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 부채는 계속해서 급격히 늘어났다.

spiral.en: a shape that winds around a center point, getting farther away as it goes

ex.en: The staircase forms a perfect spiral.
ex.ko: 그 계단은 완벽한 나선을 이루고 있다.

spiritual.en: relating to the human spirit or soul rather than physical things

ex.en: She found the retreat to be a deeply spiritual experience.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 수련회가 매우 영적인 경험이었다고 느꼈다.

spiritual.en: a religious folk song, especially one associated with African American Christian tradition

ex.en: The choir sang an old spiritual.

sport.en: a physical activity in which people compete against each other

ex.en: Swimming is my favorite sport.

sport.en: wear or display something, especially proudly

ex.en: She sported a bright red dress at the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에서 밝은 빨간 드레스를 걸쳤다.

spread.en: open something out so it covers a larger area

ex.en: She spread the map on the table.

spread.en: become or make something known or present over a wider area

ex.en: The news spread quickly through town.
ex.ko: 그 소식은 마을 전체에 빠르게 퍼졌다.

spread.cue: 빵에 발라 먹는 잼·버터 같은 것
spread.en: a soft food, such as jam or butter, spread on bread

ex.en: She put some cheese spread on her toast.
The eagle soared with its wings fully spread.
ex.ko: 그 독수리는 날개를 활짝 편 채 날아올랐다.

spy.en: a person whose job is to secretly find out information about another country or organization

ex.en: The movie is about a spy during the Cold War.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 냉전 시대의 한 스파이에 관한 이야기이다.

spy.en: catch sight of something, often suddenly

ex.en: I spied a rabbit hiding behind the bush.
ex.ko: 나는 덤불 뒤에 숨은 토끼를 언뜻 보았다.

square.en: a shape with four equal sides and four right angles

ex.en: Draw a square on the paper.

square.en: an open public area in a town, often surrounded by buildings

ex.en: Tourists gathered in the town square.

square.en: having the shape of a square

ex.en: The room has a square table.
If you square 4, you get 16.
He looked her square in the eye.

squeeze.en: to compress with violence, out of natural shape or condition

ex.en: She squeezed the lemon into the glass.

squeeze.en: an act of pressing or holding something firmly, often affectionately

ex.en: She gave his hand a gentle squeeze.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 손을 부드럽게 꼭 쥐었다.

stable.en: not likely to change or fail; firm and steady

ex.en: The patient's condition is now stable.

stable.en: a building where horses are kept

ex.en: She rode her horse back to the stable.

stable.en: keep a horse in a stable

ex.en: The groom stabled the horses for the night.
ex.ko: 마부는 밤 동안 말들을 마구간에 넣어 두었다.

ex.en: There is a stack of papers on the desk.

stack.en: arrange things in a neat pile

ex.en: He stacked the boxes neatly in the corner.

staff.en: the group of people who work for an organization

ex.en: All staff must wear name badges.

staff.en: provide a place or organization with workers

ex.en: The new hospital is staffed by experienced nurses.
ex.ko: 그 새 병원은 숙련된 간호사들로 인력이 배치되어 있다.

stage.en: a point or period in a process or in the development of something

ex.en: The project is still in an early stage.

stage.en: a raised platform where performers stand in a theater

ex.en: The singer walked onto the stage.

stage.en: organize and present an event or performance

ex.en: The students staged a play for the festival.

stake.en: (law) a right or legal share of something

ex.en: She has a stake in the company.

stake.en: risk something, such as money or reputation, on the outcome of something

ex.en: He staked his entire savings on the business.
ex.ko: 그는 전 재산을 그 사업에 걸었다.

ex.en: We are on standby for the flight.

standby.en: a person or thing that can be relied on when needed

ex.en: Rice is a standby whenever I don't know what to cook.
ex.ko: 밥은 뭘 요리할지 모를 때 늘 믿고 쓰는 대안이다.

star.en: have the most important role in a film, play, or show

ex.en: The movie stars a young actress.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 젊은 여배우가 주연을 맡았다.

star.en: a bright object in the night sky

ex.en: You can see many stars tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 밤에는 별을 많이 볼 수 있다.

star.en: a famous and successful performer, such as an actor or singer

ex.en: He became a movie star.

star.en: being the most important or best performer in a group

ex.en: She is the star player on the basketball team.

station.en: a building or place where trains or buses stop for passengers

ex.en: I'll meet you at the train station.

station.en: assign someone to a particular place for duty

ex.en: The soldiers were stationed near the border.

steep.en: rising or falling sharply; hard to climb

ex.en: They climbed a steep hill.

steep.en: (of a price or demand) unreasonably high

ex.en: $50 for a sandwich is a bit steep.

steep.en: let something sit in a liquid to bring out its flavor

ex.en: Let the tea leaves steep for five minutes.

stern.en: of a stern or strict bearing or demeanor

ex.en: She gave a stern warning.

stern.en: the back part of a ship

ex.en: Waves crashed against the stern of the boat.
My neck feels stiff.
The detective found a stiff in the alley.

still.en: continuing to happen or be true up to now

ex.en: He still lives with his parents.
It was raining, but we still went out.
Please stay still.

still.en: a single photograph taken from a movie

ex.en: The magazine printed a still from the new film.
ex.ko: 그 잡지는 새 영화의 스틸 사진을 실었다.

ex.en: She tried to still her racing heart.

stitch.en: a link or loop or knot made by an implement in knitting, crocheting, embroidery, or sewing

ex.en: She sewed with careful stitches.

stitch.en: join or fasten things together by sewing

ex.en: The nurse stitched up the wound.

stock.en: the capital raised by a corporation through the issue of shares entitling holders to an ownership interest (equity)

ex.en: Do you stock any kerosene heaters?

stock.en: used so often that it has become predictable and unoriginal

ex.en: He gave the same stock answer to every question.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 질문에 같은 상투적인 답변을 했다.

ex.en: He threw a stone into the lake.

stone.en: remove the seed or pit from a piece of fruit

ex.en: Stone the cherries before baking the pie.
ex.ko: 파이를 굽기 전에 체리 씨를 빼세요.

strand.en: a pattern forming a unity within a larger structural whole

ex.en: A strand of hair fell on her shoulder.
ex.ko: 머리카락 한 가닥이 그녀의 어깨에 떨어졌어요.

strand.en: leave someone in a place with no way to leave

ex.en: The storm left hundreds of passengers stranded at the airport.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍으로 수백 명의 승객이 공항에 발이 묶였다.

straw.cue: 음료를 마실 때 빠는 얇은 관
straw.en: a thin tube used for drinking a liquid

ex.en: She drank her juice through a straw.

straw.en: the dried stalks of wheat or other grain crops

ex.en: The farmer covered the ground with straw.

straw.en: having a pale yellow color like dried straw

ex.en: She had straw-colored hair.
She strode confidently across the stage.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신 있게 무대를 성큼성큼 걸었어요.

stride.en: a step forward in progress or development

ex.en: The team has made great strides this year.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 올해 큰 진전을 이루었다.

string.en: thin cord used for tying things

ex.en: She tied the box with a piece of string.

string.en: a thin wire on a musical instrument that makes sound

ex.en: One of the guitar strings broke.

string.en: a series of similar things that happen one after another

ex.en: There has been a string of accidents on this road.

string.en: put beads or similar objects on a string or thread

ex.en: She strung the beads to make a necklace.
ex.ko: 그녀는 목걸이를 만들기 위해 구슬을 실에 꿰었다.

structure.en: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or connected

ex.en: The structure of the sentence is quite complex.

structure.en: something that has been built, such as a building or bridge

ex.en: The bridge is an impressive steel structure.

structure.en: arrange or organize something according to a plan

ex.en: The teacher structured the lesson around three main topics.
ex.ko: 그 교사는 세 가지 주요 주제를 중심으로 수업을 구성했다.

stupid.en: not intelligent or sensible; showing a lack of thought

ex.en: It was stupid of me to forget my keys.
ex.ko: 내가 열쇠를 깜빡한 건 멍청한 짓이었다.

ex.en: Don't call your brother a stupid.

style.en: the particular way in which something is done

ex.en: I like her relaxed teaching style.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 편안한 교수 방식을 좋아한다.

style.en: a fashionable or attractive way of dressing or appearing

ex.en: She always dresses with great style.
ex.ko: 그녀는 언제나 아주 멋스럽게 옷을 입는다.

style.en: arrange hair or clothes in a particular fashionable way

ex.en: She had her hair styled for the wedding.

subject.en: a topic that is discussed, written about, or studied at school

ex.en: Math is my favorite subject.

subject.en: cause someone to experience something, usually unpleasant

ex.en: The prisoners were subjected to harsh conditions.

subtitle.en: translation of foreign dialogue of a movie or TV program

ex.en: I watched the movie with subtitles.

subtitle.en: add subtitles to a film or video

ex.en: The film was subtitled in English.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 영어 자막이 달려 있었다.

suck.en: draw into the mouth by creating a practical vacuum in the mouth

ex.en: The baby sucks on a pacifier.

suck.en: the act of drawing something in by suction

ex.en: One suck on the straw and the drink was gone.
ex.ko: 빨대로 한 번 빨자 음료가 다 없어졌다.

ex.en: The war caused great suffering.
Suffering patients waited for hours at the clinic.
ex.ko: 고통받는 환자들이 진료소에서 몇 시간이나 기다렸다.

sugar.en: a sweet substance used to make food and drinks taste sweeter

ex.en: Do you take sugar in your coffee?

sugar.en: add sugar to something to make it sweet

ex.en: She sugared her coffee before drinking it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 커피를 마시기 전에 설탕을 넣었다.

sun.en: the star that gives light and heat to the earth

ex.en: The sun rises in the east.
They sunned themselves on the beach all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그들은 오후 내내 해변에서 일광욕을 했다.

ex.en: That's a super idea!

super.en: used before an adjective to mean very (informal)

ex.en: I'm super tired today.

supernatural.en: not existing in nature or subject to explanation according to natural laws

ex.en: The movie is about supernatural events.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 초자연적인 사건들에 관한 것이에요.

supernatural.en: supernatural forces, beings, and events considered as a whole

ex.en: She has always been fascinated by the supernatural.
ex.ko: 그녀는 늘 초자연적인 것에 매료되어 왔다.

supplement.en: textual matter that is added onto a publication

ex.en: She takes a vitamin supplement.

supplement.en: add something to make up for what is missing

ex.en: He supplements his income by working weekends.
ex.ko: 그는 주말에 일을 해서 수입을 보충한다.

surface.en: the outside or top layer of something

ex.en: The lake's surface was completely still.

surface.en: to become known or noticeable after being hidden

ex.en: New evidence surfaced during the trial.

surface.en: seeming true or real only until looked at more closely

ex.en: Their friendship was only surface politeness.

surrender.en: give up or agree to forgo to the power or possession of another

ex.en: The army surrendered after the battle.

surrender.en: the act of giving up or admitting defeat

ex.en: The general announced the surrender of the enemy army.

survey.en: a set of questions asked to many people to find out their opinions

ex.en: We did a survey of student opinions.

survey.en: look carefully at the whole of something

ex.en: She surveyed the damage after the storm.
ex.ko: 그녀는 폭풍 후 피해 상황을 살펴보았다.

suspect.en: to believe that something is probably true, without being sure

ex.en: I suspect she already knows the answer.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 이미 답을 알고 있을 거라고 짐작한다.

suspect.en: to believe that someone may be guilty of something wrong

ex.en: Police suspect him of the robbery.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그가 강도 사건에 연루되었다고 의심한다.

suspect.en: a person who the police think may have committed a crime

ex.en: The suspect was arrested at the airport.

suspect.en: possibly false or dangerous; not to be trusted

ex.en: The email's link looked suspect, so she didn't click it.
ex.ko: 그 이메일의 링크가 수상쩍어 보여서 그녀는 클릭하지 않았다.

swan.en: stately heavy-bodied aquatic bird with very long neck and usually white plumage as adult

ex.en: Swans swam gracefully on the lake.

swan.en: (British informal) go somewhere in a casual or carefree way

ex.en: He just swanned off on holiday without telling anyone.
ex.ko: 그는 아무에게도 말하지 않고 그냥 유유히 휴가를 떠났다.

ex.en: She swept the floor.
With one sweep of his arm, he cleared the table.
ex.ko: 그는 팔을 한 번 휘둘러 탁자를 치웠다.

ex.en: This cake is very sweet.

sweet.en: a small piece of candy (British English)

ex.en: The children love sweets.

sweet.en: in a soft, pleasant, or loving way (informal)

ex.en: The birds sang sweet in the early morning.

swell.en: to become larger or rounder than normal

ex.en: Her ankle swelled after the fall.

swell.en: to increase gradually in size or number

ex.en: The crowd swelled to thousands.
We had a swell time at the party.
ex.ko: 우리는 파티에서 아주 좋은 시간을 보냈다.

swell.en: a slow, rolling movement of the sea's surface

ex.en: The boat rocked gently on the ocean swell.
ex.ko: 그 배는 바다의 너울에 부드럽게 흔들렸다.

tackle.en: deal with a difficult problem or task in a determined way

ex.en: The government needs to tackle the housing crisis.
ex.ko: 정부는 주택 위기 문제를 해결해야 한다.

tackle.en: try to take the ball from an opponent by knocking them down or blocking them

ex.en: The defender tackled him just before he could score.
ex.ko: 수비수는 그가 득점하기 직전에 태클을 걸었다.

ex.en: He packed his fishing tackle before heading to the lake.
ex.ko: 그는 호수로 가기 전에 낚시 도구를 챙겼다.

tax.en: money that people or businesses must pay to the government

ex.en: We pay income tax every year.

tax.en: charge a tax on income, goods, or property

ex.en: The government taxes cigarettes heavily.

taxi.en: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere

ex.en: We took a taxi to the airport.

taxi.en: (of an aircraft) move slowly along the ground before or after flying

ex.en: The plane taxied to the runway before takeoff.

teen.en: a young person between the ages of 13 and 19

ex.en: Many teens spend hours on social media.
ex.ko: 많은 십대들이 소셜미디어에 여러 시간을 보낸다.

teen.en: being between the ages of 13 and 19

ex.en: She has a teen daughter and a younger son.
ex.ko: 그녀에게는 10대 딸과 더 어린 아들이 있다.

telescope.en: an instrument that makes distant objects look bigger and closer

ex.en: He looked at the stars through a telescope.

telescope.en: crush together or collapse, one part sliding into another

ex.en: The front cars telescoped in the crash.

term.en: a word or phrase used to describe a particular thing

ex.en: 'Photosynthesis' is a scientific term.

term.en: one of the periods that a school year is divided into

ex.en: The fall term starts in September.

term.en: (usually plural) the conditions of an agreement or contract

ex.en: Please read the terms of the contract carefully.

term.en: give a particular name or description to something

ex.en: Critics termed the film a masterpiece.
I was still in school then.
We had dinner, and then we watched a movie.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁을 먹고, 그러고 나서 영화를 봤다.

then.en: used before a noun to describe someone's role or state at a past time

ex.en: The then president signed the treaty in 1990.
ex.ko: 그 당시 대통령은 1990년에 그 조약에 서명했다.

then.en: that particular time in the past or future

ex.en: We hadn't met before then.
ex.ko: 우리는 그때 이전에는 만난 적이 없었다.

thick.en: having a large distance between the two opposite surfaces of something

ex.en: She wore a thick coat in winter.

thick.en: the busiest or most intense part of an activity

ex.en: She stayed calm in the thick of the argument.
ex.ko: 그녀는 논쟁이 한창일 때도 침착함을 유지했다.

thin.en: having a small distance between opposite surfaces, or having little body fat

ex.en: He cut the bread into thin slices.

thin.en: make a liquid weaker by adding water or another liquid

ex.en: Thin the paint with a little water before using it.
ex.ko: 사용하기 전에 물을 조금 넣어 페인트를 묽게 하세요.

third.en: coming after the second in order

ex.en: He finished in third place.

third.en: one of three equal parts of something

ex.en: A third of the students failed the test.

third.en: in the third position when listing things

ex.en: First, preheat the oven. Second, mix the batter. Third, pour it into the pan.
ex.ko: 첫째, 오븐을 예열하세요. 둘째, 반죽을 섞으세요. 셋째, 팬에 부으세요.

thread.en: a fine cord of twisted fibers (of cotton or silk or wool or nylon etc.) used in sewing and weaving

ex.en: She sewed with a needle and thread.

thread.en: move carefully through a narrow or crowded space

ex.en: She threaded her way through the crowded market.
ex.ko: 그녀는 붐비는 시장을 요리조리 누비고 지나갔다.

thrill.en: the swift release of a store of affective force

ex.en: The roller coaster gave her a thrill.

thrill.en: give someone a feeling of great excitement or pleasure

ex.en: The concert thrilled the entire audience.
ex.ko: 그 콘서트는 관객 전체를 짜릿하게 만들었다.

thumb.en: the short thick finger on the side of your hand

ex.en: He hurt his thumb while chopping vegetables.

thumb.en: turn the pages of a book quickly, without reading closely

ex.en: She thumbed through the magazine while waiting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기다리는 동안 잡지를 휙휙 넘겨 보았다.

thunder.cue: 번개 뒤에 들리는 크고 우르릉거리는 소리
thunder.en: the loud noise that comes from the sky during a storm, after lightning

ex.en: The thunder scared the little dog.

thunder.en: produce the loud sound of thunder

ex.en: It thundered loudly during the storm.

timely.en: happening at exactly the right time

ex.en: Her timely advice saved the project.
He timely warned us of the danger ahead.
ex.ko: 그는 때맞춰 우리에게 다가올 위험을 경고했다.

ex.en: She tiptoed past the sleeping baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자는 아기를 지나 발끝으로 걸었어요.

tiptoe.en: standing or walking on the tips of one's toes

ex.en: She walked tiptoe past the sleeping baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자는 아기 옆을 발끝으로 걸어 지나갔다.

tire.en: to become or make someone feel weak or in need of rest

ex.en: Long meetings tire me out.

tire.en: to become bored or lose interest in something

ex.en: He never tires of telling that story.
ex.ko: 그는 그 이야기를 하는 데 결코 싫증 내지 않는다.

tire.en: a rubber covering fitted around a wheel

ex.en: One of the car's tires went flat.
ex.ko: 그 차의 타이어 하나가 펑크 났다.

title.en: the name of a book, film, song, or other work

ex.en: I can't remember the title of the movie.

title.en: a word such as 'Mr' or 'Doctor' used before someone's name

ex.en: What is her job title?

title.en: give a name or title to a book, film, or artwork

ex.en: She titled her novel 'The Quiet Storm.'
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 소설에 '고요한 폭풍'이라는 제목을 붙였다.

toast.en: bread that has been made brown and crisp by heating

ex.en: I had toast and jam for breakfast.

toast.en: an act of raising glasses and drinking to wish someone well

ex.en: Let's make a toast to the happy couple.

toast.en: raise a glass and drink to celebrate someone or something

ex.en: We toasted the newlyweds with champagne.
We had dinner together.
She seems really together for someone so young.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그렇게 어린데도 참 침착해 보인다.

toll.en: a fee levied for the use of roads or bridges (used for maintenance)

ex.en: We paid a toll on the highway.

toll.en: (of a bell) ring slowly, especially to mark an event

ex.en: The church bell tolled at noon.

tool.en: an object used to do a particular job

ex.en: A hammer is a useful tool.

tool.en: drive around casually, without a particular destination (informal)

ex.en: We tooled around town in his new car.
ex.ko: 우리는 그의 새 차를 타고 시내를 돌아다녔다.

ex.en: We reached the top of the mountain.

top.en: being the best or highest in rank

ex.en: She is a top student in her class.

top.en: be better or more than something else

ex.en: Her sales topped everyone else's this month.
ex.ko: 이번 달 그녀의 매출은 다른 모든 사람들을 능가했다.

ex.en: The prisoners were tortured.
Waiting for the test results was pure torture.
ex.ko: 시험 결과를 기다리는 것은 정말 고통스러운 일이었다.

toss.en: to throw something lightly or carelessly

ex.en: She tossed the ball to her friend.
He tossed the empty can into the trash.

toss.en: an act of throwing a coin to decide something by chance

ex.en: They decided who would go first with a coin toss.
ex.ko: 그들은 동전 던지기로 누가 먼저 할지 정했다.

tower.en: a tall, narrow building or structure

ex.en: They visited the famous tower.

tower.en: be much taller than the surrounding things or people

ex.en: The skyscraper towers over the old buildings nearby.
ex.ko: 그 고층 건물은 근처 오래된 건물들 위로 우뚝 솟아 있다.

ex.en: The tramp slept under the bridge.
The hikers tramped through the muddy trail.
ex.ko: 그 등산객들은 진흙투성이 길을 터벅터벅 걸었다.

trample.en: the sound of heavy treading or stomping

ex.en: The crowd trampled the flowers.

trample.en: walk heavily on something, crushing or damaging it

ex.en: Don't trample the flowers in the garden.

traverse.en: a horizontal beam that extends across something

ex.en: The bridge has a steel traverse.
ex.ko: 그 다리는 강철 가로대를 가지고 있어요.

traverse.en: travel across or through an area

ex.en: The climbers traversed the icy ridge carefully.

trim.en: cut a small amount off something to make it neater or the right size

ex.en: He trimmed the hedge in front of his house.

trim.en: reduce the amount or size of something

ex.en: The company trimmed its budget this year.

trim.en: having a slim, fit, and healthy-looking body

ex.en: He stayed trim by jogging every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 조깅을 해서 날씬하고 탄탄한 몸매를 유지했다.

truck.en: a large vehicle used for carrying goods

ex.en: The truck delivered furniture to the house.
The company trucks vegetables to markets every morning.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 매일 아침 트럭으로 채소를 시장에 나른다.

true.en: in agreement with fact or reality

ex.en: Is it true that you're moving?

true.en: real and genuine, not fake or pretended

ex.en: They have a true friendship.

true.en: used to admit that something is correct before adding more information

ex.en: True, the trip was expensive, but it was worth it.
ex.ko: 사실 그 여행은 비쌌지만, 그럴 만한 가치가 있었다.

trumpet.en: a brass musical instrument with a bright, loud sound

ex.en: He plays the trumpet in a jazz band.
The company trumpeted its record profits.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 자사의 기록적인 수익을 요란하게 알렸다.

ex.en: She felt a tug on her sleeve.
The dog tugged at the leash.

tumble.en: fall suddenly, especially by losing your balance or footing

ex.en: The little boy tumbled down the stairs.

tumble.en: decrease suddenly and by a large amount

ex.en: Stock prices tumbled after the announcement.

tumble.en: a sudden fall, especially by rolling or turning over

ex.en: She took a nasty tumble on the icy path.

tunnel.en: a passageway through or under something, usually underground (especially one for trains or cars)

ex.en: The train went through a tunnel.

tunnel.en: dig a passage through or under something

ex.en: The rescue team tunneled through the rubble to reach survivors.
ex.ko: 구조대는 생존자에게 닿기 위해 잔해를 뚫고 굴을 팠다.

tutor.en: a person who gives private instruction (as in singing, acting, etc.)

ex.en: She hired a math tutor for her son.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들을 위해 수학 개인 교사를 고용했어요.

tutor.en: teach someone individually, often in a particular subject

ex.en: She tutors high school students in math.
There are twelve months in a year.
There are twelve months in a year.
She is twenty years old.
She invited twenty guests to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 스무 명의 손님을 초대했다.

ex.en: There was a twinkle in his eye.

twinkle.en: shine with a bright, flickering light

ex.en: The stars twinkled in the night sky.
I have two brothers.
I have two brothers.
He was unconscious after the fall.

unconscious.en: the part of the mind containing thoughts and feelings a person is not aware of

ex.en: Freud believed dreams reveal the unconscious.

unfortunate.en: having bad luck, or causing regret

ex.en: It was unfortunate that she missed the flight.
ex.ko: 그녀가 비행기를 놓친 것은 안타까운 일이었다.

unfortunate.en: a person who has bad luck or suffers misfortune

ex.en: The charity helps unfortunates who have lost their homes.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 집을 잃은 불운한 사람들을 돕는다.

union.en: an organization formed by workers to protect their rights and interests

ex.en: The workers joined a union to negotiate better pay.
ex.ko: 근로자들은 더 나은 임금을 협상하기 위해 노동조합에 가입했다.

union.en: a group of countries or organizations joined together for a common purpose

ex.en: The European Union has many member countries.
Union workers went on strike for better pay.
ex.ko: 노동조합 근로자들이 임금 인상을 요구하며 파업했다.

unknown.en: not known about or not identified

ex.en: The cause of the fire is still unknown.
The film starred a young, unknown actor.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 젊은 무명 배우가 주연으로 나왔다.

unknown.en: a value in an equation that needs to be found

ex.en: Solve the equation to find the unknown, x.
ex.ko: 미지수 x를 구하기 위해 방정식을 풀어라.

up.en: toward a higher place or position

ex.en: She looked up at the sky.
Are you up yet?

up.en: increase or raise something, such as a price or amount (informal)

ex.en: The store upped its prices last month.

upset.en: unhappy or worried because something bad happened

ex.en: She was upset about losing her job.

upset.en: make someone feel unhappy or worried

ex.en: I didn't mean to upset you.

upset.en: knock something over so it turns upside down

ex.en: The cat upset the vase of flowers.

upset.en: a minor illness in which part of the body isn't working normally

ex.en: She had a stomach upset after the trip.

vacation.en: a period of time when you don't have to work or study

ex.en: We're going to the beach for vacation.

vacation.en: spend time away from work or school, relaxing or traveling

ex.en: They vacationed in Hawaii last summer.

vein.en: one of the tubes that carry blood from the body back to the heart

ex.en: The nurse found a vein to draw blood.

vein.en: form or mark with a pattern like veins

ex.en: The marble was veined with gold.

veteran.en: a person who has served in the armed forces, especially during a war

ex.en: The parade honored local war veterans.
ex.ko: 그 퍼레이드는 지역 참전 용사들을 기렸다.

veteran.cue: 한 분야에서 오래 경험을 쌓은 사람
veteran.en: a person with a lot of experience in a particular activity or field

ex.en: She is a veteran of the fashion industry.

veteran.en: having a lot of experience in a particular activity

ex.en: A veteran teacher led the training session.

walking.en: the activity of going somewhere on foot

ex.en: Walking is good for your health.

walking.en: close enough to reach on foot

ex.en: The station is within walking distance of our house.
ex.ko: 그 역은 우리 집에서 걸어갈 수 있는 거리에 있다.

way.en: a route or direction to get somewhere

ex.en: Can you show me the way to the station?
ex.ko: 역으로 가는 길을 알려줄 수 있어요?

way.en: a manner or method of doing something

ex.en: This is the best way to learn English.
ex.ko: 이것이 영어를 배우는 가장 좋은 방법이다.

way.en: very much; to a great extent (informal)

ex.en: This bag is way too expensive.

weather.en: the condition of the sky and air at a certain time and place

ex.en: The weather is nice today.

weather.en: (nautical) facing the direction the wind is blowing from

ex.en: The sailors moved to the weather side of the ship.

weight.en: how heavy a person or thing is

ex.en: He has lost a lot of weight recently.

weight.en: a heavy object lifted as exercise

ex.en: He lifts weights at the gym three times a week.
ex.ko: 그는 헬스장에서 일주일에 세 번 웨이트를 든다.

ex.en: Her opinion carries a lot of weight in this company.
ex.ko: 그녀의 의견은 이 회사에서 큰 비중을 차지한다.

weight.en: put a heavy weight on something to hold it down

ex.en: He weighted the tarp with rocks so it wouldn't blow away.
ex.ko: 그는 방수포가 날아가지 않도록 돌로 눌러 두었다.

well.en: in a good or satisfactory way

ex.en: She sings very well.
I don't feel well today.

well.cue: 물을 얻기 위해 판 깊은 구멍
well.en: a deep hole dug in the ground to get water, oil, or gas

ex.en: The village still uses an old stone well.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 여전히 오래된 돌우물을 사용한다.

well.en: (of a liquid) rise slowly to the surface

ex.en: Tears welled up in her eyes.

wet.en: covered or soaked with water or another liquid

ex.en: My shoes are still wet from the rain.
ex.ko: 내 신발은 비 때문에 아직 젖어 있다.

ex.en: Wet the cloth before you clean the window.
Don't leave the book out in the wet.
ex.ko: 그 책을 물기 있는 곳에 두지 마.

wheel.en: a round object that turns to allow a vehicle to move

ex.en: The car has four wheels.
ex.ko: 그 차는 바퀴가 네 개 있다.

wheel.en: the round device a driver holds to steer a vehicle

ex.en: He gripped the wheel tightly.
He wheeled around when he heard his name.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 이름을 듣고 홱 돌아섰다.

whole.en: complete, with no part left out

ex.en: She ate the whole cake by herself.
ex.ko: 그녀는 케이크를 통째로 혼자 다 먹었다.

whole.en: all of something; the entire amount

ex.en: The whole of the city was affected by the storm.
That's a whole different situation.

wild.en: living or growing in natural conditions, not controlled by people

ex.en: Wild flowers grew along the path.
The crowd went wild when the band appeared.

wild.en: a natural area or condition not affected by people

ex.en: These animals live in the wild.

wild.en: in an uncontrolled or undomesticated way

ex.en: The children were allowed to run wild in the yard.
ex.ko: 아이들은 마당에서 마음껏 뛰놀 수 있었다.

will.en: the mental power to decide and control your actions

ex.en: She has a strong will to succeed.

will.en: used before a verb to talk about the future

ex.en: It will rain tomorrow.

will.en: a legal document that says what happens to someone's property after they die

ex.en: He left everything to his children in his will.
ex.ko: 그는 유언장에서 모든 것을 자녀들에게 남겼다.

will.en: leave property to someone through a legal will after death

ex.en: She willed her house to her niece.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 집을 조카에게 유언으로 남겼다.

willing.en: ready and happy to do something

ex.en: She is willing to help whenever needed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 필요할 때면 언제든 기꺼이 도와준다.

willing.en: the mental act of making a choice or decision

ex.en: Philosophers have long debated the nature of willing.
ex.ko: 철학자들은 오랫동안 의지 작용의 본질에 대해 논쟁해 왔다.

wing.en: one of the parts of a bird, insect, or plane that is used for flying

ex.en: The eagle spread its wings.

wing.en: a part of a building that sticks out from the main part

ex.en: The hospital built a new wing for children.
ex.ko: 병원은 어린이를 위한 새 별관을 지었다.

wing.en: a group within an organization that has particular views

ex.en: He belongs to the liberal wing of the party.
The geese winged their way south for the winter.
ex.ko: 그 기러기들은 겨울을 나기 위해 남쪽으로 날아갔다.

winter.cue: 일 년 중 가장 추운 계절
winter.en: the coldest season of the year

ex.en: It often snows in winter.

winter.en: spend the winter season in a particular place

ex.en: Many birds winter in warmer countries.
ex.ko: 많은 새들이 더 따뜻한 나라에서 겨울을 난다.

wise.en: showing good judgment based on knowledge and experience

ex.en: It was wise of you to save some money.
ex.ko: 돈을 좀 저축해둔 건 현명한 일이었다.

wise.en: a way or manner of doing something (archaic, used in fixed phrases)

ex.en: In no wise should you ignore the warning.
ex.ko: 어떤 식으로든 그 경고를 무시해서는 안 된다.

word.en: a single unit of language that has meaning

ex.en: Do you know the meaning of this word?
We haven't had any word from him yet.
ex.ko: 우리는 아직 그에게서 아무 소식도 못 들었다.

ex.en: She worded the apology very carefully.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사과의 말을 아주 신중하게 표현했다.

world.cue: 지구 전체와 그 안의 모든 사람
world.en: the earth and all the people and places on it

ex.en: She wants to travel around the world.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세계 곳곳을 여행하고 싶어 한다.

world.en: involving or affecting the whole world

ex.en: She holds the world record for the marathon.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마라톤 세계 기록을 보유하고 있다.

ex.en: That's the wrong answer.
You spelled my name wrong.
ex.ko: 너 내 이름 철자를 잘못 썼어.

ex.en: It's wrong to lie to your friends.

wrong.en: something that is morally or legally incorrect

ex.en: He knows the difference between right and wrong.
She felt she had been wronged by her employer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고용주에게 부당한 대우를 받았다고 느꼈다.

yellow.en: having the color of a lemon or banana

ex.en: She wore a yellow shirt.

yellow.en: the color of a lemon or banana

ex.en: Yellow is a bright, cheerful color.
The old newspaper had yellowed over the years.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 신문은 세월이 지나면서 누렇게 변했다.

yes.en: used to give a positive answer or show agreement

ex.en: Yes, I agree with you.

yes.en: an answer or vote that shows agreement

ex.en: The vote resulted in more yeses than noes.
ex.ko: 투표 결과 반대보다 찬성이 더 많았다.

ex.en: I met her yesterday.

yesterday.en: the day before today, or the recent past

ex.en: Fashion trends of yesterday are coming back.

young.en: not old; in an early stage of life

ex.en: They have three young children.
The bird fed its young every few hours.
ex.ko: 그 새는 몇 시간마다 새끼에게 먹이를 먹였다.

ad-lib.en: say or do something without preparing it in advance

ex.en: The actor ad-libbed a joke when he forgot his line.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 대사를 잊어버리자 즉흥적으로 농담을 했다.

ad-lib.en: something said or done spontaneously without being planned

ex.en: Her ad-libs got more laughs than the script.
ex.ko: 그녀의 애드리브가 대본보다 더 많은 웃음을 자아냈다.

ad-lib.en: done without preparation or a script

ex.en: His ad-lib remarks got a big laugh.
ex.ko: 그의 즉흥적인 발언은 큰 웃음을 자아냈다.

ex.en: The living conditions in the camp were appalling.

appalling.en: a shocking or horrifying occurrence (rare noun usage)

ex.en: Historians still write about the appalling of that winter.
ex.ko: 역사가들은 여전히 그해 겨울의 참혹한 사태에 대해 기술한다.

bulk.en: the majority or largest part of something

ex.en: The bulk of the work is already done.

bulk.en: the large size or mass of something

ex.en: The furniture's bulk made it hard to move.
ex.ko: 그 가구는 부피가 커서 옮기기 힘들었다.

ex.en: His muscles bulked under the tight shirt.
ex.ko: 딱 붙는 셔츠 아래로 그의 근육이 불룩해졌다.

chisel.en: carve or shape wood or stone using a chisel

ex.en: The sculptor chiseled the marble into a statue.
ex.ko: 그 조각가는 대리석을 끌로 깎아 조각상을 만들었다.

chisel.en: cheat someone out of money or property (informal)

ex.en: He was accused of chiseling investors out of their savings.
ex.ko: 그는 투자자들의 저축금을 사취했다는 혐의를 받았다.

chisel.en: a tool with a sharp flat edge used for cutting or shaping wood or stone

ex.en: The sculptor used a chisel to carve the statue.
ex.ko: 그 조각가는 끌을 사용해 조각상을 조각했다.

forfeit.en: lose or give up a right, privilege, or possession as a penalty

ex.en: Passengers who miss check-in forfeit their tickets.
ex.ko: 체크인 시간을 놓친 승객은 항공권을 몰수당한다.

forfeit.en: something that is lost or given up as a penalty for breaking a rule or agreement

ex.en: The contract includes a forfeit for late delivery.
ex.ko: 그 계약에는 배송 지연 시 위약금 조항이 포함되어 있다.

ex.en: His forfeit deposit was never returned.
ex.ko: 몰수된 그의 보증금은 결코 돌려받지 못했다.

frolic.en: to play and jump around happily

ex.en: The puppies frolicked in the yard.

frolic.en: cheerful, playful activity done for fun

ex.en: The puppies' frolic in the yard made everyone smile.
ex.ko: 강아지들이 마당에서 뛰노는 모습에 모두가 미소 지었다.

integral.en: necessary and essential to make something complete

ex.en: Trust is integral to any good relationship.

integral.en: (mathematics) the result of integrating a function

ex.en: The student calculated the integral of the function.
ex.ko: 그 학생은 그 함수의 적분값을 계산했다.

notable.en: important or interesting enough to deserve attention

ex.en: There has been a notable increase in remote work since the pandemic.
ex.ko: 팬데믹 이후 재택근무가 눈에 띄게 증가했다.

ex.en: Several notable scientists attended the conference.
ex.ko: 여러 저명한 과학자들이 그 학회에 참석했다.

ex.en: Several local notables attended the opening ceremony.
ex.ko: 몇몇 지역 유명 인사들이 개회식에 참석했다.

proposition.en: a suggestion or plan put forward for consideration

ex.en: The company received an interesting business proposition.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 흥미로운 사업 제안을 받았다.

proposition.en: a statement that can be judged true or false

ex.en: The mathematician proved the proposition using a simple proof.
ex.ko: 그 수학자는 간단한 증명으로 그 명제를 증명했다.

proposition.en: make a sexual suggestion to someone, often unwelcome

ex.en: She complained that a stranger had propositioned her at the bar.
ex.ko: 그녀는 바에서 낯선 사람이 자신에게 노골적인 제안을 했다고 항의했다.

recess.en: a break from lessons or work

ex.en: The kids played outside during recess.

recess.en: a temporary pause in the activities of a court, parliament, or other formal body

ex.en: The court was in recess until the following Monday.

recess.en: a small area in a wall that is set back from the main surface

ex.en: A small statue stood in a recess in the wall.
ex.ko: 벽의 움푹 들어간 공간에 작은 조각상이 놓여 있었다.

recess.en: stop a meeting or session temporarily

ex.en: The court recessed until Monday morning.

reissue.en: publish or release something again, often with small changes

ex.en: The publisher decided to reissue the novel with a new cover.
ex.ko: 그 출판사는 새 표지로 그 소설을 재발행하기로 했다.

reissue.en: something that is published or released again, unchanged

ex.en: The band released a reissue of their first album.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 첫 앨범의 재발매반을 출시했다.

romp.en: play in a lively, energetic way

ex.en: The puppies romped around the yard all afternoon.

romp.en: win a game or competition easily

ex.en: The home team romped to victory, 5-0.
The home team won in a romp, 6 to 0.

silhouette.cue: 빛을 등지고 서 있을 때 보이는 검은 윤곽
silhouette.en: the dark outline of a person or object seen against a lighter background

ex.en: We could see the silhouette of the mountains against the sunset.
ex.ko: 우리는 노을을 배경으로 산의 실루엣을 볼 수 있었다.

silhouette.en: show the outline of something against a light background

ex.en: The mountains were silhouetted against the sunset.

sulphur.en: a yellow chemical element with a strong smell, found near volcanoes

ex.en: The hot spring smelled strongly of sulphur.
ex.ko: 그 온천에서는 유황 냄새가 강하게 났다.

sulphur.cue: 말린 과일 등을 황으로 훈증 처리하다
sulphur.en: treat something, such as dried fruit, with sulphur to preserve it

ex.en: The apricots were sulphured before drying.
ex.ko: 그 살구는 건조되기 전에 황으로 훈증 처리되었다.

virtuoso.en: a person with exceptional skill, especially in music

ex.en: She is a violin virtuoso who has performed all over the world.
ex.ko: 그녀는 전 세계에서 공연해온 바이올린 거장이다.

virtuoso.en: showing extremely skillful technique or mastery

ex.en: She gave a virtuoso performance on the piano.

wheeze.cue: 숨을 쉴 때 쌕쌕 소리가 나며
wheeze.en: breathe with difficulty, making a whistling sound

ex.en: The old man wheezed as he climbed the stairs.

wheeze.en: a rough, whistling sound made while breathing

ex.en: His asthma gave him a persistent wheeze.
ex.ko: 그의 천식 때문에 계속 쌕쌕거리는 소리가 났다.

whir.en: make a soft, continuous sound like something spinning fast

ex.en: The fan whirred quietly in the corner of the room.

whir.en: a continuous buzzing or whirring sound made by something moving fast

ex.en: The whir of the fan helped her fall asleep.
ex.ko: 선풍기의 윙윙거리는 소리가 그녀를 잠들게 했다.

wrack.en: cause someone to suffer greatly, especially from pain or guilt

ex.en: He was wracked with guilt after the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고 이후 죄책감에 몹시 시달렸다.

wrack.en: a state of complete ruin or destruction

ex.en: The old mansion fell into wrack and ruin.
